prompt:
Pen-and-watercolor illustration in the style of a contemporary illustrated ski guidebook. Confident black ink contour lines drawn with a fine pen describe every form, then filled with richly saturated watercolor washes — dense, layered, with visible brushstrokes and patches of unpainted white paper showing through within shapes. Visible cross-hatching and stippling in shadows. Crisp edges where ink meets paint. Detailed and storybook-illustrated, not loose or impressionistic. Palette: full-color but rooted in alpine blues, warm earth browns, deep evergreens, fresh snow whites, with a single saturated red accent where the subject calls for it. CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS: no caption text below or beside the image, no title text, no hand-lettered labels of any kind. The painting must fill the canvas edge-to-edge with NO white deckled border, NO painted frame, NO unpainted margin — the page provides its own framing. Background color must be a uniform warm cream paper #faf5e9 unless the subject explicitly calls for a scene.

Match the painting style, palette, and texture of the attached reference images exactly.

Subject: Top-down schematic ski-piste base map of the Aspen ski area in Colorado, painted in pen and watercolor in the same style as the attached reference, BUT WITH NO TEXT AND NO ANNOTATIONS WHATSOEVER. Four distinct snow-capped ski mountains spread across the top half of the composition from left to right — broadest on the left, smaller in the left-center, steep and dramatic in the right-center, narrower on the right — each rendered with shaded ridgelines, snow-white tops, and faint pale corridors suggesting ski runs winding down each peak's flanks. Dark stippled conifer forest on the lower slopes. A small painted village of red-roofed wooden buildings nestled at the bottom center. Cream paper ground, deckled paper border. Square composition. CRITICAL: absolutely no lettering, no labels, no mountain names, no text of any kind, no pin markers, no numbered circles, no dotted lines, no route arrows — all of those are added afterward as a digital overlay. The painted base must be entirely free of writing or graphic symbols. Just pure painted terrain.
